summary: Homegrown Atlanta is the residential real estate practice of REALTOR Aaron Moen at Keller Williams Realty Intown Atlanta, offering buyer's-agent and home-listing services across the Atlanta metro. It serves first-time buyers, move-up and downsizing sellers, and out-of-state relocators who want plain-English guidance and a trusted relationship over a fast, transactional experience. Revenue is commission-based and project-by-project, with long-term value coming from repeat transactions and referrals across the 5–7 year homeownership cycle.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new lead (questionnaire submission or list join) to first value fast by guiding them to book their free consultation with Aaron.
campaigns.trigger: Home buyer/seller questionnaire submitted, or new contact joins the list. Send email 1 within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New contacts; branch on questionnaire answer (buyer vs. seller vs. relocator) to lead with the relevant 'aha'.
campaigns.rationale: There is no automated welcome sequence today and the consult is the brand's entire activation moment — a warm, instant first email captures intent while it's hottest and sets Aaron's friendly, no-pressure tone before a competitor's Instagram ad does.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations, and deliver the one quick win: booking the free, no-commitment consultation with Aaron.
emails.subject: Welcome to Homegrown — let's get cooking on your Atlanta move
emails.preview: Your first step is easier than you think (and it's free).
emails.cta: Book your free consult with Aaron
keyPoints: Thank them for reaching out and reassure: no pressure, no cost, no commitment to the first consult
Reference their stated goal from the questionnaire (buying, selling, or relocating) so it feels personal
Frame the 30-minute consult as the fastest way to turn an overwhelming market into a clear plan
Real reply-to from Aaron — 'hit reply, a real person (me) reads every one' — establishes the trusted-advisor relationship

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert warm leads who downloaded a questionnaire or browsed but haven't yet booked into a booked consultation, by neutralizing the 'I'm not ready / market timing' objection.
campaigns.trigger: Lead engaged (questionnaire submitted, pages visited) but no Calendly booking after the welcome email; expiry-style nudge running in parallel.
campaigns.segment: Active, un-booked leads; branch by buyer vs. seller and by stated timeline (now vs. 'just looking').
campaigns.rationale: The booked consult is the single revenue event and the top objection here is timing-related, not price — so the highest-leverage email is one that removes the 'now isn't the right time' fear with a zero-commitment, plain-English conversation.

emails.purpose: Directly address the market-timing objection and lower the friction to booking by reframing the consult as advice, not a sales pitch.
emails.subject: Not sure it's the right time to buy or sell in Atlanta? Let's just talk
emails.preview: 30 minutes, zero commitment — and you'll finally know where you stand.
emails.cta: Grab a time on Aaron's calendar
keyPoints: Validate the hesitation: most people feel unsure about timing — that's exactly what the consult is for
Reframe the consult as a no-obligation gut-check on YOUR situation, not a push to list or buy today
Short, specific proof point: a recent client who 'wasn't ready' and left the call with a clear, stress-free plan
Soft-handle 'shopping around': encourage it, and offer to be the second opinion that explains the rest plainly

campaigns.name: Closing Anniversary & Referral
campaigns.objective: Keep past clients warm across the 5–7 year gap between moves, stay top-of-mind, and generate referrals and repeat transactions.
campaigns.trigger: Closing-date anniversary (annual); also usage milestone signals like a kid heading to college or rising local home values.
campaigns.segment: Past clients (closed buyers and sellers); branch by tenure and likely next move (upsize, downsize, invest).
campaigns.rationale: The brand's core churn risk is being forgotten between transactions — the intake names past-client retention as half the primary goal, so an annual, genuinely useful anniversary touch is what protects the highest-value asset Aaron has.

emails.purpose: Celebrate the home anniversary, deliver real value (a current home-value update), and open the door to referrals without being pushy.
emails.subject: Happy homeiversary! 🏡 A year in your Atlanta home
emails.preview: A little thank-you — plus what your home is worth now.
emails.cta: Get your free home value update
keyPoints: Warm, personal note marking one year since closing — make it feel like a friend remembering, not a CRM
Deliver tangible value: a free, no-strings current market value estimate for their specific home
Remind them Aaron is still in their corner for any home question, not just the next sale
Gentle two-sided referral ask: 'know someone feeling overwhelmed by the market? Send them my way and I'll take great care of them'

campaigns.name: Booking, Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended consultations and recover the ones that slip, since attendance is the revenue event.
campaigns.trigger: Calendly consultation booked; upcoming appointment; no-show event.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled buyer or listing consultation with Aaron.
campaigns.rationale: Calendly sends only bare reminders today; a branded, warm pre-consult reminder cuts no-shows (~35% on average) and makes the highest-stakes appointment in the funnel actually happen.

emails.purpose: Day-before reminder that confirms the time, builds anticipation, removes prep anxiety, and offers a friction-free reschedule.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow — here's how to prep for our chat
emails.preview: Bring your questions. Aaron brings the plain-English answers.
keyPoints: Confirm date, time, and how to join (call/video/in-person) with an add-to-calendar link
Set expectations so they show up relaxed: 'no homework required — just bring whatever's on your mind'
Suggest one or two easy things to think about (budget comfort zone, ideal neighborhoods or timeline)
One-click reschedule link so a conflict becomes a new time instead of a no-show

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move a cold questionnaire lead toward consult-readiness over a longer cycle by educating and building trust until they're ready to book.
campaigns.trigger: Home buyer or seller questionnaire submitted but lead not yet sales-ready (no consult booked, low engagement).
campaigns.segment: Leads below the consult-ready threshold; branch by buyer vs. seller and by the questionnaire's stated timeline.
campaigns.rationale: The questionnaires already capture rich intent but get no follow-up — a stage-matched nurture turns those dormant submissions into future consults instead of letting them go cold and forget Aaron.

emails.purpose: Deliver value off the back of the questionnaire and frame the real problem (the process feels overwhelming because no one has explained it plainly).
emails.subject: Got your questionnaire — here's what most Atlanta buyers get wrong next
emails.preview: The real reason the market feels overwhelming (and the simple fix).
emails.cta: When you're ready, book a free consult
keyPoints: Acknowledge their submission and recap what it tells Aaron about their goals (buyer/seller, timeline)
Teach one genuinely useful thing — e.g., why the home loan / pre-approval step matters before house-hunting in a fast market
Frame the core problem: overwhelm comes from not having a guide, not from the market itself
Low-pressure CTA with a 'no rush' tone — book whenever ready, and reply with questions anytime in the meantime
